all_candidates.topic: Should large retailers pay UPI transaction fees?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The Indian government is considering reintroducing a small Merchant Discount Rate (MDR) for large UPI retailers, as reported on July 18, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Is India's IT sector prepared for AI-driven job shifts?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Reports from July 18-19, 2026, highlight '1 lakh job losses' trending in tech and estimates of 25,000-35,000 job cuts in India's IT industry this year due to AI.

all_candidates.topic: Is CBSE's new two-attempt exam system beneficial?
all_candidates.news_anchor: CBSE introduced a new two-attempt system for Class 10 and 12 board exams in 2026 to ease student stress and improve assessment, as reported on July 16, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Should companies prioritize reskilling over AI-driven layoffs?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Recent reports on July 19, 2026, indicate that AI-driven restructuring in India's IT sector is leading to job cuts, prompting discussions on reskilling.

all_candidates.topic: Should consumers have a choice for E20 petrol?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Delhi CM Arvind Kejriwal opposed E20 petrol and demanded consumer choice on July 14, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Should government regulate AI platforms more strictly?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The Indian government intensified scrutiny of AI-driven platforms and questioned WhatsApp's proposed username feature over fraud concerns on July 13, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Is using JEE scores for IIM UG admissions fair?
all_candidates.news_anchor: JEE scores can now be used for admission to IIM's undergraduate programmes after Class 12, as reported on July 19, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Is India's IT model outdated for the AI era?
all_candidates.news_anchor: A report on July 19, 2026, highlighted that India's IT sector's pyramid model, relying on repetitive tasks, is threatened by AI.

all_candidates.topic: Should election voter forms be simplified?
all_candidates.news_anchor: India's poll panel tweaked voter forms on July 12, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Should India invest in flying bus urban transport?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Union Minister Nitin Gadkari proposed the concept of flying buses for urban transport in India on July 16, 2026, to reduce congestion.

all_candidates.topic: Is Delhi's new private school rule beneficial?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Delhi eased private school admission rules, replacing mandatory locality-need assessment with self-certification, reported on July 19, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Should India strictly enforce forced-labor import bans?
all_candidates.news_anchor: India prohibited the import of goods produced using forced labour on July 14, 2026, with the new clause in its Foreign Trade Policy becoming effective within 30 days.

all_candidates.topic: Should private companies build India's nuclear power?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The Naveen Jindal Group initiated discussions with global tech providers for its proposed nuclear power projects in India, following government legal amendments facilitating private participation, reported on July 19, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Will data centre growth create enough new tech jobs?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Union Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh stated on July 12, 2026, that India's data centre capacity is projected to grow significantly, potentially creating one lakh engineering jobs in AI systems.

all_candidates.topic: Should India prioritize BRICS urban mobility hub initiatives?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The 3rd BRICS Transport Ministers' Meeting on July 11-12, 2026, adopted a declaration including cooperation on Urban Mobility Hubs.

all_candidates.topic: Should India prioritize sustainable aviation fuel development?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The 3rd BRICS Transport Ministers' Meeting on July 11-12, 2026, adopted a declaration including cooperation on Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF).

all_candidates.topic: Should India push for more circular infrastructure?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The 3rd BRICS Transport Ministers' Meeting on July 11-12, 2026, adopted a declaration including cooperation on circularity in infrastructure.

all_candidates.topic: Is 'One Nation, One Time' essential for India's future?
all_candidates.news_anchor: India is set to roll out the 'One Nation, One Time' initiative, ending reliance on foreign time sources for critical infrastructure synchronization, as announced on July 16, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Should India regularly adjust fuel export windfall taxes?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The Central government adjusted windfall taxes on diesel and jet fuel exports (hiked) and petrol exports (cut) on July 16, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Will GSTN's AATO update improve business compliance?
all_candidates.news_anchor: GSTN is deploying an upgraded AATO functionality from July 1, 2026, to automatically update aggregate turnover and improve consistency, as reported on July 13, 2026.
